Test_Objective: Cover HSPHY register access through APB interface from PGA, with LDO in off state
Test_Scenario: Put LDO into off state, by configuration all lanes to have receiver detection fail. Here is how to do it:+DEFAULT_FUSE : Use this plusarg to allow port manager to use default LOFL fuses, which is \'h5. This will allow PCIe to start receiver detection.+CFG_SLEEP_MAP_TO_RTOFF=1: Use this plusarg to enable LDO to map sleep to rtoff state.+PCIE_RXDET_FAIL: Use this plusarg to force rxstatus = \'h0 at PCIe top. This will simulate no PCIe detected scenario (receiver detection fail).+PCIE_CONFIG +PCIE_FUNC_DIS: Use this plusarg to program clk and power gate setting for all PCIe (active and inactive) ports. The key here is to enable PHY power gating setting in PCIe, so that PCIe controller would move the LTSSM state to DETECTQUIETPG. In this state, the powerdown value should be \'h3, deeppm_req should be asserted to 1. HSPHY would be able to enter SLEEP state.Use +HSPHY_LDO_OFF to ensure LDO has enter OFF state (o_ldo_obs_phy_main_amp_en == 0)For other test steps, please refer pch_hsphy_register_access_test
Coverage: HSPHY register access through APB interface from PGA, with LDO in off state
SelfCheck: PCIe BFM protocol checker Data Coherency Check Test self check

Test_Objective: Ensure PTM flow is working fine between PCIe and PMA(Note: This test is only added in G5S3. In PCD/PCH model, it is covered under HH chapter)
Test_Scenario: Enable PTM in PCIe port.Set PTMECFG.IOSFMADP = 2\'b10.Set PTMCAPR.PTMRSPC = 1\'b1.Set PTMCTLR.PTME = 1\'b1.Once enabled, PCIe controller should start the PTM sync handshake with the ARU agent. (PMA)Run the following checks in parallel:Check #1:Check for tsc_sync pulse. This is a direct wire between PMA and PCIe.Check for synccomp msg with tag = 0. This msg indicates PTM sync handshake between PCIe and PMA has completed. tag = 0 means success synccomp. Refer IOSF spec for more details.Check #2:Check localsync timestamp. Ensure every localsync timestamp from PMA is captured by PCIe. This check is meant to run indefinitely due to there could be more than 1 localsync message.Check #3:check tsc valid. Once synccomp success is received, PCIe should asserts tsc valid for the respective port if PTM for that port is enabled.All checks can be terminated once Check#1 and Check #3 have completed.Request PCIe BFM to initiate PTM Request. Repeat this for total of 3 times to complete the PTM dialogue. Ensure PCIe controller responded with PTM respond. For 2nd and 3rd response, PCIe should send PTM respond with Data.
Coverage: PTM sync handshake between PCIe and PMA.
SelfCheck: PCIe BFM protocol checker Data Coherency Check Test self check

Test_Objective: Verify dynamic power gating/clock gating, trunk clock gating, PHY PLL shutdown in L1.STD flow.
Test_Scenario: 1. Configure BFM L1 substates ability settings, and program controller\'s registers for L1 substates configurations (Refer to BWG).2. Configure BFM to send desirable device LTR and program controller\'s registers on LTR thresholds values (Refer to HAS on L1 substates entry criteria based on LTR). 3. Configure BFM to initiate L1.STD entry triggered via ASPM L1 mode. 4. BFM shall send PM_Active_State_Request_L1 DLLP repeatedly. Controller response with PM_Request_Ack DLLP repeatedly until electrical idle observed5. Poll BFM link state and ensure BFM link state transitioned to L1_IDLE before timeout.6. Check controller L1CKREQCTLSM FSM is in (IDLE) state.7. Check controller LPIF - Link Layer Request to Logical Physical Layer for state change is in correct request state (\'b0100). Refer LPIF spec for encodings.8. Check controller LPIF - Physical Layer to Link Layer Status indication in expected state (\'b0100). Refer LPIF spec for encodings.9. Check controller dynamic power gating/clock gating, FIA Trunk clock gating, and PHY PLL shutdown. For L1.STD: i. TX common mode and squelch detection remains ON ii. prim_clkreq/ack DEASSERTED iii. side_clkreq/ack DEASSERTED iv. xtal_clkreq/ack DEASSERTED v. osc_clkreq/ack remains ASSERTED vi. g2/g3 pllen/pllok DEASSERTED (g2/g3 depending on implementation and gen speed supported, cover end to end checking between PCIe <-> FIA <-> PHY) vii. g2/g3 coreclken/ack DEASSERTED (g2/g3 depending on implementation, cover end to end checking between PCIe <-> FIA <-> PCG) viii. Controller powerdown is in PS4 (SNPS PHY in P1.CPM (PLL Shutdown), HSPHY in PS4) ix. PHY PLL* force_ack DEASSERTED (PLLA/B; fast PLL/slow PLL depending on PHY type and implementation. PLL shutdown by controller dynamically through pllen de-assertion) x. LDO state in ACTIVE (If LDO required) xi. PIA ext_fet_ack remains ASSERTED (Power FET still ON)10. Send upstream memory cycles to exit L1.STD, BFM will assert CLKREQB. Send downstream memory cycles. Check data cohenrency for upstream and downstram memwr and memrd cycles.11. Upon L1.STD exit, checks: i. TX common mode and squelch detection are ON ii. prim_clkreq/ack ASSERTED iii. side_clkreq/ack remains DEASSERTED iv. xtal_clkreq/ack remains DEASSERTED v. osc_clkreq/ack are ASSERTED vi. g2/g3 pllen/pllok are ASSERTED (g2/g3 depending on implementation and gen speed supported, cover end to end checking between PCIe <-> FIA <-> PHY) vii. g2/g3 coreclken/ack are ASSERTED (g2/g3 depending on implementation, cover end to end checking between PCIe <-> FIA <-> PCG) viii. Controller powerdown is in PS0 ix. PHY PLL* force_ack remains DEASSERTED or ASSERTED (PLLA/B; fast PLL/slow PLL; depending on gen speed trained, PHY type and implementation. PLL enable by controller dynamically through pllen assertion. Refer to integration HAS to check the actual implementation. Checkers varied per project and HSIO topology) x. LDO state in ACTIVE (If LDO required)xi. PIA ext_fet_ack are ASSERTED (Power FET ON)

Test_Objective: Verify DPC triggered during HPR/Sx flow will not cause hang in PM flow.
Test_Scenario: Execute below in parallel Trigger cold reset entry (reuse PMC HPR seq).Trigger DPC after random delay (refer to DPC tests on register programming and possible trigger events)TResetPrep can be measured from jester.log and PXP sideband tracker. It is the time taken when PMC Sx/Reset sequence starts, until PXP receives RESETPREP msg. Run multiple seeds and take an average value (Note: remove outliers) TR1 , TR2 , TR3 are random delays before trigger DPC R1: Trigger DPC before PXP received ResetPrep.  R2: Trigger DPC slight before PXP received ResetPrep OR Trigger DPC slight after PXP received ResetPrep R3: Trigger DPC after PXP received ResetPrepPMA sends Reset_Prep to PCIe portsAfter controller receives RESETPREP msg, controller will send PME_TURN_OFF to PCIe BFMIf DPC has been triggered, PME_TURN_OFF handshake must be handled as if the port has acknowledged the PME_TURN_OFF request and have entered L23_Rdy state.Controller discard PME_TURN_OFF msg internally and consider PME_TO_ACK has been received.Controller will send the RESETPREPACK back to PMA.Once PMA receives RESETPREPACK, PMA will send FORCEPWRGATEPOK and system should proceed to enter cold reset.Check cold reset flow entry is successful (PMC HPR seq self check)Trigger cold reset exit.Execute SHPC post reset initialization flow.Rerun PCIe config sequence.Send upstream and downstream mem transactions to verify the link is active and functional.

Test_Objective: Verify S0i2p2 flow with SRM zero restore disabled.(Note: Zero restore enabled means, whenever SRM sees the register value is same as default value(usually all zero), then SRM will skip restoring the register. On the other hand, zero restore disabled means, SRM will always restore the register regardless what is the register value.)
Test_Scenario: Run S0i2p2 with all PCIe lanes set to NO_OWNER and NO PCIe device connected. You can reuse pch_s0i2p2_L1p2_pcie test so that we don\'t have to rewrite a new test from scratch. L1 configuration and flow will be automatically skipped if there is no PCIe device connected.Please make sure hwsnr register programming is bypassed in subsystems/pcie/verif/common/seqlib_vegan/pch_pcie_r_REG_cpg_aseq.svh. This is to create a scenario where the first SnR register (addr 0x5000) is matching RTL default value(all zeros) so that SRM will skip the restore for this SnR register if zero restore feature is (accidentally)enabled. Then this would cause the test to fail. Because PCIe needs to see the first SnR register (addr 0x5000) being restored regardless what is the value.For example, you can use a plusarg to guard hwsnr_program().if (!$test$plusargs("PCIE_BYPASS_HWSNR")) begin        hwsnr_program ();end    Checks: In failing case: You will see SRM skip MWr to SnR register with address 0x5000 during VNN restore flow. For example: You will see the first MWr starts with address 0x5004. (after SETIDVALUE msg)In passing case: You will see SRM sends MWr to address starting with 0x5000 during VNN restore flow. (even if the data is all 0)For PTLP, create dedicated json file for each PCIe controller (PXPA,B and C)For example, PXPA:

The flow is similar to S3 or warm reset from PCIe perspective. ]For S3i2p2, chipsetinit comes in to do reconfiguration(all configuration should have been done by minset sxix config sequence). The flow is similar to S3 or warm reset from PCIe perspective. ]Once system has entered S3, execute pch_pma_vnn_snr_seq. Test_Objective: Verify controller dpc/edpc triggering (via upstream TLP) and error reporting. In this test, PCIe will send interrupt as MSI.
Test_Scenario: Configure DPC Handling during SERM (PCIEALC.DPCHSERM – default 0). This register is expected to be set before enabling DPC. Set PCIEALC.DPCHSERM = 0Set DPC Capability Register (Should be already default to 1, check OSXML) Set DPCCAPR.RPEFDPC = 1 (RP Extensions For DPC) Set DPCCAPR.PTLPEBS = 1 (Poisoned TLP Egress Blocking Supported) Root Ports that support RP Extensions for DPC must set this bit. Set DPCCAPR.DPCSTS = 1 (DPC Software Triggering Supported) Root Ports that support RP Extensions for DPC must set this bit. Set DPCCAPR.DLAECSS = 1 (DL_Active ERR_COR Signaling Supported) Root Port that supports RP Extensions for DPC must set this bit. Set DPC Extended Capability Register Set DPCECH.CID = ‘h1D Set DPCECH.CV = 1 Enable DPC, with DPC triggered when detects an unmasked uncorrectable error or when receives an ERR_NONFATAL or ERR_FATAL message.   Set DPCCTLR.DPCTE = ‘b10  Set DPC ERR_COR enable, an ERR_COR message will be generated each time the DPC Trigger Status bit transitions from Clear to Set Set DPCCTLR.DPCECE = 1 Set DPC Interrupt Enable, to enables the generation of an interrupt (INTx or MSI) to indicate that DPC has been triggered. Set DPCCTLR.DPCIE = 1 Set MC.MSIE = 1 to generate MSI. Do not set this if INTx send out as sideband.  Note that If MSI is enable, INTx will be disable No need to cover SCI and SMI: still true in TTLH [Poh Thiam] For RZLW, DPC SCI and DPC SMI do not need to cover, this is for DPC exception but we don’t have the software hook, we didn\'t implement the software functionality for this. For SCI: DPCECTL.DPCTSCIEN For SMI: DPCECTL.DPCTSMIEN PXP send SCI and SMI to PMA, refer to PMA HAS on the interrupt management. For SCI: Test must cover the pathway as a whole i.e PXP -> PMA -> PMC -> ITSS -> OS (PVC BFM) For SMI: Test must cover the pathway as a whole i.e PXP -> PMA -> PMC -> D2D SBBSet DPCCTLR.DPCCC to 0/1 (randomized) to controls the Completion Status for completions formed during DPC: 0b: Completer Abort (CA)  1b: Unsupported Request (UR)  Set DPCCTLR.DLAECE to 1 to enables the port to signal ERR_COR when the link transitions to the DL_Active state. Enable Correctable Error Reporting Set DCTL.CEE = 1 Set Poisoned TLP Egress Blocked Mask to 0 (If DPC is enabled and Poisoned TLP Egress Blocked error is unmasked, DPC must be triggered by the poisoned TLP) Set UEM.PTLPEBM = 0 Send upstream mem write traffics, then send any one of the below cycles to trigger DPC. Randomize the trigger event: Upstream ERR_FATAL message OR Upstream ERR_NONFATAL message OR Upstream MEM_WR with EP bit set to 1 (Or other uncorrectable errors e.g Malformed TLP, ECRC errors) After send any one of ERR_FATAL/ERR_NONFATAL/MEM_WR with EP set, continue send few upstream mem write cycles, check all these subsequent upstream TLPs will be silently discarded, flag error if cycle forwarded to fabrics. Check the ERR_FATAL / ERR_NONFATAL that triggered DPC is silently discarded and not forwarded to fabric. While triggering DPC event, use PIBA subscriber to check if VLW (NMI bit set) message is received. This is the end to end result. Elaborate more here*  While triggering DPC event, start a thread to check controller sent out interrupt when DPC is triggered. Cover end to end check.The interrupt will be issue as MSI.  When both MSI and VLW (NMI bit set) messages are generated in response to a given DPC trigger event, the MSI must push the VLW (NMI bit set) message. Implement a checker on this. While triggering DPC event, start a thread to wait for DPCSR.DPCTS = 1 with a timeout, flag error when DPC Trigger Status is not set after timeout. Once DPCSR.DPCTS is set, read DPCSR.DPCTR (DPC Trigger Reason) and check DPCSR.DPCTR = ‘b10 if Upstream ERR_FATAL message triggered DPC DPCSR.DPCTR = ‘b01 if Upstream ERR_NONFATAL message triggered DPC DPCSR.DPCTR = ‘b00 if Upstream MEM_WR/RD with EP bit set to 1 triggered DPC. If DPCSR.DPCTR = ‘b10/’b01, check the Requester ID from the message is recorded in DPCESIDR.DPCESID (DPC Error Source ID - check if the ID matched to the BFM ports that sent the message) While triggering DPC event, poll for DPCSR.DPCRPB = 1 (DPC RP Busy) with a timeout, flag error if DPC RP Busy bit is not set after timeout Wait this bit to be cleared by hardware (DPCSR.DPCRPB = 0). Set a timeout. While triggering DPC event, poll for BFM link state = DISABLED Use exiting task: pcie_poll_TRAIN_DISABLED() While triggering DPC event, poll for LSTS.LA = 0 with a timeout, flag error if Link Active bit is not 0 after timeout [Optional – Covered by IP] While triggering DPC event, send continuous downstream MEM_RD. Stop after controller started to return completion with UR/CA. This is to create scenario to have pending completions when DPC is triggered. After DPC triggered,  if there is any pending completion for previous transmitted non-posted cycle, check: controllers return completion with UR if DPCCTLR.DPCCC is set to 1 controllers return completion with CA if DPCCTLR.DPCCC is set to 0 if there is no any pending completion: Check controller discard all yet to be transmitted non-posted TLP silently Flag error if controller transmitted these TLP to the link Once DPC has been triggered, all other downstream posted requests and downstream completions must be silently discarded. Software clear DPC status bit. Write 1 to DPCSR.DPCTS.  Do not clear DPC status bit while RP busy bit is still 1. Link retrain will happen. Poll for link back to L0 Use existing task pcie_poll_L0_ACTIVE() [RZL RAS HAS] Software can use Data Link Layer State Changed interrupts, DL_Active ERR_COR signaling, or both, to signal when the Link reaches the DL_Active state again. [PCIe HAS] DL_ACTIVE ERR_COR signaling is managed independently of Data Link Layer State Changed interrupts, and it is permitted to use both mechanisms concurrently. [RZL RAS HAS] It is recommended that Operating Systems use the Data Link Layer State Changed Interrupts method for signaling when DL_ACTIVE changes state. The DL_ACTIVE_ERR_COR signaling method represents a subset of the same events and is primarily intended for use by platform SW when it needs to be notified to do Downstream Port configuration or provide Firmware First services. [PCIe HAS] For a given DL_ACTIVE event, if a Port is going to send both an ERR_COR Message and an MSI transaction, then the Port must send the ERR_COR Message prior to sending the MSI transaction. Use PIBASubscriber to check ERR_COR is received, an indication of link transitions to the DL_Active state Check DSTS.CED is not set to 1. This event is not handled as an error for this case. Use PIBASubscriber to check MSI is received, an indication of link transitions to the DL_Active state Send upstream and downstream traffic after link back to L0.
SelfCheck: self check

Test_Objective: Verify controller dpc/edpc triggering (via RPPIO error) and error reporting. In this test, PCIe will send interrupt as MSI.
Test_Scenario: Configure DPC Handling during SERM (PCIEALC.DPCHSERM). This register is expected to be set before enabling SERM or DPC. During SERM, when DPC is triggered Root Port will generate ECC_COR on IOSF Primary and would not generate MSI. Set PCIEALC.DPCHSERM = 1 During SERM, when DPC is triggered Root Port will not send ERR_COR message on IOSF Primary but would generate MSI instead on IOSF Primary Set PCIEALC.DPCHSERM = 0 Set DPC Capability Register (Should be already default to 1, check OSXML) Set DPCCAPR.RPEFDPC = 1 (RP Extensions For DPC) Set DPCCAPR.PTLPEBS = 1 (Poisoned TLP Egress Blocking Supported) Root Ports that support RP Extensions for DPC must set this bit. Set DPCCAPR.DPCSTS = 1 (DPC Software Triggering Supported) Root Ports that support RP Extensions for DPC must set this bit. Set DPCCAPR.DLAECSS = 1 (DL_Active ERR_COR Signaling Supported) Root Port that supports RP Extensions for DPC must set this bit. Set DPC Extended Capability Register Set DPCECH.CID = ‘h1D Set DPCECH.CV = 1 Enable DPC, with DPC triggered when detects an unmasked uncorrectable error or when receives an ERR_NONFATAL or ERR_FATAL message.   Set DPCCTLR.DPCTE = ‘b10  Set DPC ERR_COR enable, an ERR_COR message will be generated each time the DPC Trigger Status bit transitions from Clear to Set Set DPCCTLR.DPCECE = 1 Set DPC Interrupt Enable, to enables the generation of an interrupt (INTx or MSI) to indicate that DPC has been triggered. Set DPCCTLR.DPCIE = 1 Set MC.MSIE = 1 to generate MSI  Note that If MSI is enable, INTx will be disable No need to cover SCI and SMI [Poh Thiam] For RZLW, DPC SCI and DPC SMI do not need to cover, this is for DPC exception but we don’t have the software hook, we didn\'t implement the software functionality for this. For SCI: DPCECTL.DPCTSCIEN For SMI: DPCECTL.DPCTSMIEN PXP send SCI and SMI to PMA, refer to PMA HAS on the interrupt management. For SCI: Test must cover the pathway as a whole i.e PXP -> PMA -> PMC -> ITSS -> OS (PVC BFM) For SMI: Test must cover the pathway as a whole i.e PXP -> PMA -> PMC -> D2D SBB Set DPCCTLR.DPCCC to 0/1 (randomized) to controls the Completion Status for completions formed during DPC: 0b: Completer Abort (CA)  1b: Unsupported Request (UR)  Set DPCCTLR.DLAECE to 1 to enables the port to signal ERR_COR when the link transitions to the DL_Active state. Enable Correctable Error Reporting Set DCTL.CEE = 1 Set Poisoned TLP Egress Blocked Mask to 0 Set UEM.PTLPEBM = 0 Set RP PIO severity register fields to 1  If the associated Severity bit is Set, the error is handled as uncorrectable, triggering DPC (assuming that DPC is enabled) and signalling this event with a DPC interrupt and/or ERR_COR (if enabled).  If the associated Severity bit is clear, the error is handled as an Advisory Non-Fatal Error (without triggering DPC) and signalled with ERR_COR (if enabled). Set RPPIOVR.CURCSV = 1 (Configuration Unsupported Request Completion Severity) Set RPPIOVR.CCACSV = 1 (Configuration Completer Abort Completion Severity) Set RPPIOVR.CCTSV = 1 (Configuration Completion Timeout Severity) Set RPPIOVR.IOURCSV = 1 (I/O Unsupported Request Completion Severity) Set RPPIOVR.IOCACSV = 1 (I/O Completer Abort Completion Severity) Set RPPIOVR.IOCTSV = 1 (I/O Completion Timeout Severity) Set RPPIOVR.MURCSV = 1 (Memory Unsupported Request Completion Severity) Set RPPIOVR.MCACSV = 1 (Memory Completer Abort Completion Severity) Set RPPIOVR.MCTSV = 1 (Memory Completion Timeout Severity) Check if RP PIO error trigger DPC. Send downstream CFG_WR/CFG_RD/IO_WR/IO_RD/MEM_RD cycle. Randomize the type of cycle to send downstream. Use PCIe BFM callback function to randomize to have one of the responses below: Return Unsupported request completion Return Completer abort completion Disable BFM to send completion While triggering RP PIO error DPC event, use PIBA subscriber to check if ERR_COR message is received. While triggering RP PIO error DPC event, start a thread to wait for DPCSR.DPCTS = 1 with a timeout, flag error when DPC Trigger Status is not set after timeout. Once DPCSR.DPCTS is set, read DPCSR.DPCTR and check DPCTR read value is ‘b11 indicating DPC was triggered due to a reason that is indicated by the DPC Trigger Reason Extension field (DPCTE). Read and check DPCSR.DPCTR = ‘b11 indicating DPC was triggered due to RP PIO error. Read DPCSR.RPPIOFEP to get the first error pointer. The value of this field identifies a bit position in the RP PIO Status register (RPPIOSR) Read and check RPPIOSR[DPCSR.RPPIOFEP] tally with the error conditions (UR/CA/completion timeout)  E.g. if the IO_RD is getting UR, then RPPIOSR[8] (RPPIOSR.IOURCS) I/O Unsupported Request Completion Status is set to 1. Read and check RPPIOHLR_DW* (RP PIO Header Log DW* Register) register whether TLP info matching to the cycle type that trigger the error. While triggering RP PIO error DPC event, poll for DPCSR.DPCRPB = 1 with a timeout, flag error if DPC RP Busy bit is not set after timeout Wait this bit to be cleared by hardware (DPCSR.DPCRPB = 0). Set a timeout. While triggering RP PIO error DPC event, poll for BFM link state = DISABLED Use exiting task: pcie_poll_TRAIN_DISABLED() While triggering RP PIO error DPC event, poll for LSTS.LA = 0 with a timeout, flag error if Link Active bit is not 0 after timeout Software clear DPC status bit. Write 1 to DPCSR.DPCTS.  Do not clear DPC status bit while RP busy bit is still 1. Link retrain will happen. Poll for link back to L0 Use existing task pcie_poll_L0_ACTIVE() [RZL RAS HAS] Software can use Data Link Layer State Changed interrupts, DL_Active ERR_COR signaling, or both, to signal when the Link reaches the DL_Active state again. [PCIe HAS] DL_ACTIVE ERR_COR signaling is managed independently of Data Link Layer State Changed interrupts, and it is permitted to use both mechanisms concurrently. [RZL RAS HAS] It is recommended that Operating Systems use the Data Link Layer State Changed Interrupts method for signaling when DL_ACTIVE changes state. The DL_ACTIVE_ERR_COR signaling method represents a subset of the same events and is primarily intended for use by platform SW when it needs to be notified to do Downstream Port configuration or provide Firmware First services. [PCIe HAS] For a given DL_ACTIVE event, if a Port is going to send both an ERR_COR Message and an MSI transaction, then the Port must send the ERR_COR Message prior to sending the MSI transaction. Use PIBASubscriber to check ERR_COR is received, an indication of link transitions to the DL_Active state Check DSTS.CED is not set to 1. This event is not handled as an error for this case. Use PIBASubscriber to check MSI is received, an indication of link transitions to the DL_Active state Send upstream and downstream traffic after link back to L0.Notes:The RP PIO SysError register provides a means to generate a System Error when an RP PIO error occurs. If an unmasked RP PIO error is detected while its associated bit in the RP PIO SysError register is set, a System Error is generated.
